name: mobile_list_available_devices
description: List all available devices. This includes both physical mobile devices and mobile simulators and emulators. It returns both Android and iOS devices.

name: mobile_list_fleet_devices
description: List devices available in the remote fleet

name: mobile_allocate_fleet_device
description: Reserve a device from the remote fleet

platform.description: The platform to allocate a device for

name: mobile_release_fleet_device
description: Release a device back to the remote fleet

device.description: The device identifier to release back to the fleet

name: mobile_list_apps
description: List all the installed apps on the device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_launch_app
description: Launch an app on mobile device. Use this to open a specific app. You can find the package name of the app by calling list_apps_on_device.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

packageName.description: The package name of the app to launch

locale.description: Comma-separated BCP 47 locale tags to launch the app with (e.g., fr-FR,en-GB)

name: mobile_terminate_app
description: Stop and terminate an app on mobile device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

packageName.description: The package name of the app to terminate

name: mobile_install_app
description: Install an app on mobile device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

path.description: The path to the app file to install. For iOS simulators, provide a .zip file or a .app directory. For Android provide an .apk file. For iOS real devices provide an .ipa file

name: mobile_uninstall_app
description: Uninstall an app from mobile device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

bundle_id.description: Bundle identifier (iOS) or package name (Android) of the app to be uninstalled

name: mobile_get_screen_size
description: Get the screen size of the mobile device in pixels

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_click_on_screen_at_coordinates
description: Click on the screen at given x,y coordinates. If clicking on an element, use the list_elements_on_screen tool to find the coordinates.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

x.description: The x coordinate to click on the screen, in pixels

y.description: The y coordinate to click on the screen, in pixels

name: mobile_double_tap_on_screen
description: Double-tap on the screen at given x,y coordinates.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

x.description: The x coordinate to double-tap, in pixels

y.description: The y coordinate to double-tap, in pixels

name: mobile_long_press_on_screen_at_coordinates
description: Long press on the screen at given x,y coordinates. If long pressing on an element, use the list_elements_on_screen tool to find the coordinates.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

x.description: The x coordinate to long press on the screen, in pixels

y.description: The y coordinate to long press on the screen, in pixels

duration.description: Duration of the long press in milliseconds. Defaults to 500ms.

name: mobile_list_elements_on_screen
description: List elements on screen and their coordinates, with display text or accessibility label. Do not cache this result.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_press_button
description: Press a button on device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

button.description: The button to press. Supported buttons: BACK (android only), HOME, VOLUME_UP, VOLUME_DOWN, ENTER, DPAD_CENTER (android tv only), DPAD_UP (android tv only), DPAD_DOWN (android tv only), DPAD_LEFT (android tv only), DPAD_RIGHT (android tv only)

name: mobile_open_url
description: Open a URL in browser on device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_swipe_on_screen
description: Swipe on the screen

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

x.description: The x coordinate to start the swipe from, in pixels. If not provided, uses center of screen

y.description: The y coordinate to start the swipe from, in pixels. If not provided, uses center of screen

distance.description: The distance to swipe in pixels. Defaults to 400 pixels for iOS or 30% of screen dimension for Android

name: mobile_type_keys
description: Type text into the focused element

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

submit.description: Whether to submit the text. If true, the text will be submitted as if the user pressed the enter key.

name: mobile_save_screenshot
description: Save a screenshot of the mobile device to a file

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

saveTo.description: The path to save the screenshot to

name: mobile_take_screenshot
description: Take a screenshot of the mobile device. Use this to understand what's on screen, if you need to press an element that is available through view hierarchy then you must list elements on screen instead. Do not cache this result.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_set_orientation
description: Change the screen orientation of the device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_get_orientation
description: Get the current screen orientation of the device

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

name: mobile_start_screen_recording
description: Start recording the screen of a mobile device. The recording runs in the background until stopped with mobile_stop_screen_recording. Returns the path where the recording will be saved.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.

output.description: The file path to save the recording to. If not provided, a temporary path will be used.

timeLimit.description: Maximum recording duration in seconds. The recording will stop automatically after this time.

name: mobile_stop_screen_recording
description: Stop an active screen recording on a mobile device. Returns the file path, size, and approximate duration of the recording.

device.description: The device identifier to use. Use mobile_list_available_devices to find which devices are available to you.
